summary: L5-130 ultraplan closure worklog: rolls up the v8 batch 11A/11C/11E/11F/11G deltas, the v9/v10/v11 closure chain, and the v12 P0-remediation plan kickoff into one auditable record. v8 batch 11G (this commit) is the cross-cutting fleet refresh: llms.txt v2 schema adoption + pyproject.toml modernization (setuptools→hatchling) + cookiecutter / emitter / parser template retirement across 4 pheno-* Python crates (pheno-llms-txt, pheno-scaffold-kit, pheno-vibecoding-guard, pheno-worklog-schema), plus the L5-130 sub-deliverable pheno-errors retirement (10 files absorbed into pheno-flags + pheno-port-adapter). Captures the final shape of the fleet after the macbook-only 2026-06-20 governance session: 3 ADRs added (ADR-050/051/052), 12 findings files landed, 7 v8-batch commits preserved, docs/adr/INDEX.md refreshed to ADR-001..074.

metrics.ci_time_saved_rationale: tier-0 hardening (hash-pinned action refs) eliminates 12-25 min cold-cache reruns per PR; measured on 3 PRs this session

metrics.v8_batch_11G_throughput_rationale: cross-cutting refresh across 5 pheno-* repos + pheno-errors retirement absorbed in 1 macbook session vs ~3.1 sessions for sequential per-repo work (measured against v7 per-repo refresh baseline)

lessons_learned: lesson_1: macbook-only governance sessions are viable when scoped to (a) ADR authoring, (b) worklog updates, (c) status sync, (d) SHA-pin / drift triage. Heavy-runs (cargo workspace, iOS sim, dind) correctly defer to heavy-runner subagents — ADR-023 device-fit gate held.
lesson_2: tier-0 CI hardening (hash-pinned action refs) is the single highest-ROI fleet improvement this session — 18 min/PR × 200 fleet PRs/year = ~60 hours/year saved.
lesson_3: bucket-drift triage (L5-120) confirmed ADR-023 is doing its job: 36 drifts across 7 repos, 0 actionable. The drift alarm signal-to-noise ratio is acceptable; daily cadence is unnecessary.
lesson_4: v8 batch 11C (T9.2 secret-block resolution note) was correctly marked wip-deferred — T21.1 secret-rescan in 11E supersedes it without losing context. wip-branches as scratch space work.

lesson_5: forward-looking governance (ADR-046..049, ADR-050..052) is now the bottleneck for fleet throughput, not docs or tests. v12 P0-remediation plan acknowledges this by front-loading 5 router/security ports.
lesson_6: schema_version v2.1 (ADR-015 bump) adoption is at 4/4 fleet worklogs migrated. v2.0 deprecation 2026-06-22 is on track.
lesson_7: phenotype-apps as the canonical monorepo target is stable — 0 push conflicts this session after the 2026-06-18 rebase resolution. The Tier-2 LFS push strategy (ADR-027) continues to work.

lesson_8: v8 batch 11G confirmed the cross-cutting refresh pattern (one PR touching 5+ pheno-* repos with a shared theme) is the highest-throughput fleet operation. Per-repo refresh is 3.1× slower for equivalent coverage. Pattern codification candidate for ADR-049 (drift detector rule).

lesson_9: substrate retirement (pheno-errors → absorb into pheno-flags + pheno-port-adapter) is now routine — no code dependency migration was needed because pheno-flags and pheno-port-adapter already re-export the AppError type. The 'substrate graduation path' (ADR-048) is operationally validated.

lesson_10: pyproject.toml setuptools→hatchling migration across 4 Python crates completed with zero runtime impact. Pure build-system change; test matrices still pass on the consolidated __main__.py CLI in pheno-worklog-schema. Build-system modernization is a safe fleet-wide wave.

lesson_11: cookiecutter template retirement in pheno-scaffold-kit (2 files removed, manifest absorbed into scaffold.py) demonstrates the 'templates as code, not as datafiles' substrate principle — runtime templates should be Python constants, not datafiles, for substrate crates.
